phrasal_verbs.meaning: make sb not want to do or not like sth
be almost a particular time, number, age, etc
continue happening or doing sth; do sth after doing sth else
begin to like; begin to do sth regularly
go/come to sb's house to visit them
develop in a particular way or have a particular result
appear unexpectedly or without making a firm arrangement

A.title: Cooking verbs
A.instructions: Complete each sentence with a cooking verb from the box.

questions.sentence: ...... the chicken pieces in hot oil until they're golden brown.
...... the onions finely before adding them to the sauce.
...... the cake in the oven at 180°C for about 30 minutes.
...... some cheese over the top of the pasta before serving.
...... the egg whites until they form stiff peaks.
...... the bread into thick pieces and toast it lightly.
...... all the ingredients together in a large bowl until smooth.
...... the vegetables in the oven with olive oil, salt and pepper.
...... the fish for about five minutes on each side.
...... the sauce continuously to prevent it from sticking to the bottom.
...... the pasta in salted water for ten minutes.
I particularly like Mexican and Indian **kitchen / cuisine**.
**Frozen / Freezing** fish is just as tasty as fresh fish.
My mum's the best **cooker / cook** in the world!
Once the **kettle / teapot** has boiled, pour the boiling water over the jelly cubes.
Excuse me. Could we have the **catalogue / menu**, please?
Boil the eggs for three minutes in a saucepan on the **grill / hob / oven**.
Any meat that's kept in the **fridge / freezer** should be defrosted thoroughly before cooking.
Grandma's having her new **chef / cooker** delivered next week.
Chilli con carne is one of my favourite **plates / bowls / saucers / dishes**.
Are you going to get your suit dry-cleaned for the Carlton's **dinner / lunch** party tomorrow night?
My friend Sally's a true **vegetable / vegetarian / vegan**, so she doesn't eat meat, fish or even any milk products like cheese!
Let's get a Chinese **takeaway / fast** food tonight.
**Fizzy / Soft** drinks are gassy because they've got carbon dioxide in them.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: I've taken (1) ...... not booking a table in my real name for these restaurant reviews...
Earlier in the evening, some friends had come (2) ...... for a chat and mentioned La Clara.
We decided just to drop (3) ...... to see if there was a free table.
There wasn't - always a risk if you (4) ...... up without booking, of course - but we decided to wait.
Having arrived at about 9.30, it was getting (5) ...... for eleven before we were finally seated.
while we were having a drink at the bar, we ran (6) ...... the food critic of a rival newspaper.
unfortunately our meal turned (7) ...... to be far from wonderful.
The sauce tasted like the cream had (8) ...... off.
Fifteen minutes after my mushrooms had arrived, the waiter came over to say that they had (9) ...... out of salmon.
He went (10) ...... to say that they would be closing at midnight.
The head chef of La Clara likes to try (11) ...... one new dish every day.
the waiter kept (12) ...... coming over to see if we had finished.
I'll leave (13) ...... a description of the dessert.
I don't want to (14) ...... anyone off trying La Clara...

D.title: Matching (Where do these texts appear?)
D.instructions: Match each text (1-10) with the place where it appears (A-J).

questions.sentence: Only heat-resistant ceramic and glass dishes should be used for oven baking/roasting. **C** in the instructions for an electric cooker
A homemade chunky and succulent beefburger, filled with mozzarella cheese and served with a side salad and baked potato. **F** on a menu
Wash the carrots and slice. Set to one side. Boil the potatoes for six minutes or until slightly soft to the touch. **D** in a recipe book
Open seven days a week. Ideal for wedding receptions, birthday parties and other special occasions. **G** in an advertisement for a restaurant

Large plates, dishes and bowls should be loaded into the bottom rack. Glasses should be placed upside down on the top rack. Cutlery should be placed in the cutlery container. **A** in the instructions for a dishwasher
Are you fed up with trying to lose weight and failing? Doreen Brown asks top nutritionists how we can lose that fat, and not put it back on again. **I** in an article on dieting

Make a note of all the things you have to do before your guests arrive... If you're planning to cook the meal yourself... make sure you choose a menu you know well. **J** in an article on organising a dinner party
Whites and delicate items should be washed separately. **B** in the instructions for a washing machine
I ask you all to raise your glasses so we can drink to my wonderful great-grandfather, ninety-seven years young today! **H** in a toast at a celebration
Vegetarian and Vegan Products. **E** on a sign in a health food shop

E.title: Word patterns (gap fill)
E.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Everyone complimented her ...... the wonderful buffet she'd laid on.
The problem with drinks like that is they're full ...... sugar.
I can't choose ...... Death by Chocolate or fruit salad.
I'm not going to tell you what's for supper. You'll just have to wait ...... see.
She's generally regarded ...... being the best cookery book writer of her generation.
Most people associate English food ...... fish and chips and shepherd's pie.
Karen's very careful ...... how much salt she has.
There's a lack ...... good restaurants round here.
The meat was well cooked, but the sauce was totally lacking ...... flavour.

F.title: Verb patterns
F.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verb in brackets.

questions.sentence: I tend not ...... (cook) very often during the week as I don't have time.
Do you remember ...... (go) to that wonderful little taverna on Skiathos last summer?
I must remember ...... (buy) some bread on the way home.
Why don't you offer ...... (help) with the washing-up?
I'd suggest ...... (get) a takeaway rather than cooking.
Would you be willing ...... (lend) me a hand with the pudding?

G.title: Word formation (text)
G.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: Good cooking is always a strange ...... (MIX) of science and art and you certainly have to be fairly ...... (CREATE) if you want to come up with your own recipes.
All cooking also demands a fair amount of ...... (PREPARE) and this is doubly true when you're producing ...... (ORIGIN) dishes.
You'll be ...... (SURPRISE) by how honest people can be when it comes to food.
I've had people tell me my latest dish is ...... (DISGUST) and ...... (THOROUGH) inedible!
Just smile ...... (SWEET) and thank them for their valuable opinion.
And never forget that when you're waiting ...... (ANXIOUS) to hear whether or not your 'masterpiece' is a success and they suddenly show their ...... (APPRECIATE) you'll realise it was all worth it.
My tips: home- ...... (GROW) vegetables are always the tastiest.
Every good cook needs a top- quality ...... (MIX). It's an essential piece of kitchen equipment, not a luxury.
Make sure you've got lots of good- quality food storage ...... (CONTAIN).
And, finally, remember that designing a new dish is not about ...... (SAFE). It's about taking risks, and learning from your mistakes.
